content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both need encode() in core.py - shared file, unavoidable. To minimize conflicts: I'll add a 'transformers' kwarg to encode()'s signature (added as the LAST keyword arg) and apply it as the very first statement in the method body (before the allowed_special/disallowed_special logic), so text is transformed before special-token checks. I will not touch encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, encode_batch's core logic beyond passing through if needed. Please add your return_positions param also as the LAST kwarg (after mine, or note your position) and try to keep your body changes near the return statement so our diffs don't collide on the same lines. I'll implement now and push; we'll merge branches at the end per protocol - happy to resolve any conflicts manually then.
